text.title: Go HTTP Handlers (Gin / Echo / Chi / stdlib) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go HTTP Handlers (Gin / Echo / Chi / stdlib) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: ONE framework per service; mixed routers across files are FORBIDDEN.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal Go reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Handlers MUST be methods on a struct holding dependencies (services, loggers), never package-level funcs with globals.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Hexagonal architecture. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Request DTOs MUST carry binding/validate tags; controller binds + validates before calling service.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: go-playground/validator docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Responses MUST be typed structs (never `map[string]any` from a handler).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Errors MUST be returned to middleware (`c.Error(err)`) that renders RFC 7807; handlers never write JSON for errors.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: error-handling methodology. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Tests MUST be table-driven over `httptest` with the actual router; mock the service interface.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Effective Go testing. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Handlers MUST pass `r.Context()` / `c.Request.Context()` to service calls; never `context.Background()`.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go context docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
